case_name: WILK, JOANNE v. JAMES, M.D., DAVID M.

summary.brief: WILK, JOANNE v. JAMES, M.D., DAVID M.. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff commenced this medical malpractice and wrongful death action seeking damages for the conscious pain and suffering, and death of Steven R. Wilk (decedent) as a result of the alleged failure by defendants to diagnose and treat decedent’s aortic dissection in a timely manner.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff submitted the affidavit of a physician who is board certified in emergency medicine, in which the physician opined that aortic dissection is a 'life-threatening' condition and should be promptly ruled out through further testing where, as here, the patient presents with a constellation of symptoms that are typical of that condition.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.text: The death certificate revealed that the immediate cause of death was a 'cerebral infarct with herniation[,] . . . due to or as a consequence of . . . shock with intestinal ischemia[,] . . . due to or as a consequence of . . . aortic dissection.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The nurse practitioner who initially assessed decedent upon his arrival at the emergency room testified at her deposition that she reviewed the triage note to obtain information about the history of decedent’s onset of pain and that it did not indicate that the pain had started suddenly at 9:30 a.m. that morning.
evidence_index.speaker: Nurse practitioner

evidence_index.text: The order denied the motions of defendants David M. James, M.D., Kaleida Health, doing business as Millard Fillmore Health System-Three Gates Circle Hospital, Sadir Alrawi, M.D., Mercy Ambulatory Care Center, Catholic Health System, Inc., and Buffalo Emergency Associates, LLP for summary judgment dismissing the amended complaint and all cross claims against them.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We conclude that, although defendants met their initial burden on their respective motions, plaintiff raised triable issues of fact whether defendants deviated from the accepted standards of medical care and whether those deviations caused decedent’s injuries and ultimate death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff’s expert further opined that, on February 13 and 15, 2004, James departed from good and acceptable medical practice by, inter alia, failing to elicit a proper medical history from decedent and failing to include and pursue aortic dissection as a differential diagnosis for decedent.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.text: On February 13, 2004, decedent was transported by ambulance to the emergency room operated by defendant Kaleida Health, doing business as Millard Fillmore Health System-Three Gates Circle Hospital (Kaleida), and was treated by defendant David M. James, M.D. and Kaleida’s staff.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The ambulance record indicated that decedent’s 'chief complaint' was 'severe back pain' that, according to the 'subjective assessment' entry on that record, started at 9:30 a.m. and felt like someone 'hit [him with a] baseball bat.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff’s expert further stated that James’s departures from the accepted standards of medical care were a substantial factor in causing decedent’s injuries and his eventual death, and thereby raised triable issues of fact with respect to causation.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.text: Decedent was discharged by James 30 minutes later with a 'diagnosis' of 'sciatica.' Forty-four minutes later, while waiting for his wife to pick him up from the emergency room, decedent experienced 'excruciating sudden [right] flank and [left] abdominal pain[]' and returned to the emergency room.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In sum, we conclude that plaintiff raised issues of fact sufficient to defeat the motions for summary judgment dismissing the amended complaint and all cross claims against defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports severe back pain that started suddenly at 9:30 a.m., feeling like being hit with a baseball bat. However, there is a conflicting report that the pain started 2 days ago.

focused_exam.back: Sharp, constant pain in lower back, increased with movement, radiating down both legs

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Back pain - differential includes thoracic spine strain
critical_findings: Sudden onset of severe back pain

requestables.name: CT scan with contrast
requestables.description: CT scan to rule out aortic dissection

reveal.decision_impact: Critical for accurate diagnosis and timely intervention

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with severe back pain
decision_points.prompt: What diagnostic test should be ordered to rule out aortic dissection?

options.clinical_reasoning: Standard test to rule out aortic dissection
Less effective for ruling out aortic dissection

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed pain was due to thoracic spine strain

expected_action_court.description: CT scan with contrast should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established CT is standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to order CT scan with contrast led to missed diagnosis of aortic dissection
explanation.what_should_have_happened: CT scan with contrast should have been performed to confirm or rule out aortic dissection

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed cerebral infarct with herniation due to aortic dissection

key_findings: Failure to diagnose and treat aortic dissection in a timely manner
Causation established - earlier diagnosis would have prevented death

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: CT would have shown aortic dissection

factual_timeline.event: Patient arrives at ED with severe back pain

tests_performed.result: Moderate degenerative changes, osteopenia, anterior wedging
